script.description: JS script to run on the page

tools.name: application-info
tools.description: Get comprehensive application information including PHP version, Laravel version, database engine, and all installed packages with their versions. You should use this tool on each new chat, and use the package & version data to write version specific code for the packages that exist.

tools.name: browser-logs
tools.description: Read the last N log entries from the BROWSER log. Very helpful for debugging the frontend and JS/Javascript

entries.description: Number of log entries to return.

tools.name: database-connections
tools.description: List the configured database connection names for this application.

tools.name: database-query
tools.description: Execute a read-only SQL query against the configured database.

query.description: The SQL query to execute. Only read-only queries are allowed (i.e. SELECT, SHOW, EXPLAIN, DESCRIBE).

database.description: Optional database connection name to use. Defaults to the application's default connection.

tools.name: database-schema
tools.description: Read the database schema for this application. Returns table names, columns, indexes, and foreign keys. Use "summary" mode first to get an overview (table names with column types only), then call again without "summary" and with a "filter" to get full details for specific tables. Params: "summary" (default false) - returns only table names and column types; "database" - connection name; "filter" - substring match on table names; "include_column_details" (default false) - adds nullable, default, auto_increment, comments; "include_views" (default false); "include_routines" (default false) - stored procedures, functions, sequences.

summary.description: Return only table names and their column types. Use this first to understand the database structure, then request full details for specific tables using "filter". Defaults to false.

database.description: Name of the database connection to dump (defaults to app's default connection, often not needed)

include_views.description: Include database views. Defaults to false.

include_routines.description: Include stored procedures, functions, and sequences. Defaults to false.

include_column_details.description: Include full column metadata (nullable, default, auto_increment, comments, generation). Defaults to false.

tools.name: get-absolute-url
tools.description: Get the absolute URL for a given relative path or named route. If no arguments are provided, you will get the absolute URL for "/"

path.description: The relative URL/path (e.g. "/dashboard") to convert to an absolute URL.

route.description: The named route to generate an absolute URL for (e.g. "home").

tools.name: last-error
tools.description: Get details of the last error/exception created in this application on the backend. Use browser-log tool for browser errors.

tools.name: read-log-entries
tools.description: Read the last N log entries from the application log, correctly handling multi-line PSR-3 formatted logs and JSON-formatted logs. Only works for log files.

entries.description: Number of log entries to return.

tools.name: search-docs
tools.description: Search for up-to-date version-specific documentation related to this project and its packages. This tool will search Laravel hosted documentation based on the packages installed and is perfect for all Laravel ecosystem packages. Laravel, Inertia, Pest, Livewire, Filament, Nova, Tailwind, and more. You must use this tool to search for Laravel-ecosystem docs before using other approaches. The results provided are for this project's package version and does not cover all versions of the package.

queries.description: List of queries to perform, pass multiple if you aren't sure if it is "toggle" or "switch", for example

packages.description: Package names to limit searching to from application-info. Useful if you know the package(s) you need. i.e. laravel/framework, inertiajs/inertia-laravel, @inertiajs/react

token_limit.description: Maximum number of tokens to return in the response. Defaults to 3,000 tokens, maximum 1,000,000 tokens. If results are truncated, or you need more complete documentation, increase this value (e.g.5000, 10000)

resources.name: application-info
resources.description: Comprehensive application information including PHP version, Laravel version, database engine, all installed packages with their versions in the application.
